headline: Buy bitcoin in the UK
subheadline: FCA-registered crypto exchanges compared — GBP deposits via Faster Payments, HMRC-friendly tax reporting, and trusted platforms.
introCopy: The UK crypto market has matured significantly with FCA oversight and clear HMRC tax guidance. Whether you're buying your first bitcoin or building a diversified portfolio, choosing an FCA-registered exchange protects your money and simplifies your tax return.
guideContent:
FCA registration

Since January 2020, crypto exchanges must be registered with the FCA under the Money Laundering Regulations. Unregistered exchanges cannot legally serve UK customers. Always verify your exchange's FCA registration status.

Depositing GBP

Faster Payments is the best option — free, instant, and supported by most exchanges. Open Banking is increasingly available for instant deposits. Card purchases work but carry 1-3% fees.

Tax rules

HMRC treats crypto as an asset subject to Capital Gains Tax. You have an annual CGT allowance. Each disposal (sell, trade, spend) is a taxable event. Keep detailed records and consider using crypto tax software for your Self Assessment.
disclaimer: BTCUK.io provides independent comparisons for UK crypto users. Cryptocurrency trading involves risk. This is not financial advice.
affiliateDisclosure: Some links are affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

items.name: Kraken
items.description: Kraken has full FCA registration and offers GBP deposits via Faster Payments. Competitive fees, a wide selection of assets, and a strong security track record make it a top choice for UK users.

items.name: Coinbase
items.description: Coinbase is FCA-registered and offers a simple, beginner-friendly experience for UK users. GBP deposits via Faster Payments, a wide coin selection, and excellent educational resources.

items.name: Bitstamp
items.description: Founded in 2011, Bitstamp is one of the oldest exchanges globally. FCA-registered with GBP support, transparent fee structure, and a strong reputation for reliability.

items.name: Uphold
items.description: Uphold lets you trade crypto, precious metals, and national currencies from a single account. FCA-registered with GBP support. The AutoPilot feature enables recurring purchases.
features: Crypto, metals, and fiat in one app

faqs.q: Is crypto legal in the UK?
faqs.a: Yes. Crypto is legal in the UK. Exchanges must be registered with the FCA under the Money Laundering Regulations. However, the FCA banned the sale of crypto derivatives (CFDs, futures, ETNs) to retail consumers.

faqs.q: How is crypto taxed in the UK?
faqs.a: HMRC treats crypto as an asset subject to Capital Gains Tax. You pay CGT on gains above your annual allowance when you sell, trade, or spend crypto. Mining and staking income may be subject to Income Tax. Keep records for your Self Assessment.

faqs.q: Can I use Binance in the UK?
faqs.a: Binance has had a complex regulatory history in the UK. Check current FCA registration status before using. Alternatives like Kraken, Coinbase, and Bitstamp are fully FCA-registered and may be safer choices for UK users.

faqs.q: What's the cheapest way to buy bitcoin in the UK?
faqs.a: Deposit GBP via Faster Payments (free on most exchanges) and use limit orders on the exchange's pro/advanced platform. Kraken and Bitstamp offer the lowest trading fees. Avoid card purchases which add 1-3% fees.
